description: Figure: P-loop catalytic motif comparison PTEN vs human/zebrafish auxilin
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

rows = [
 ("PTEN (active PTPase)",      "H  C  K A G K G  R", "C-X5-R  (canonical P-loop)", "ACTIVE"),
 ("Human DNAJC6 / auxilin",    "H  C  L D G  R",     "C-X3-R  (contracted loop)", "PSEUDO"),
 ("Zebrafish dnajc6",          "T  C  S D G  R",     "C-X3-R  (contracted; His->Thr)", "PSEUDO"),
]
fig,ax=plt.subplots(figsize=(11,3.2)); ax.axis('off')
ax.set_title("PTP catalytic P-loop (signature H-C-X5-R) — PTEN vs auxilin orthologs",fontsize=12,weight='bold')
y=0.8
ax.text(0.01,0.95,"Protein",weight='bold'); ax.text(0.30,0.95,"P-loop motif",weight='bold')
ax.text(0.55,0.95,"Spacing",weight='bold'); ax.text(0.86,0.95,"Verdict",weight='bold')
for name,motif,spacing,verd in rows:
    col='green' if verd=='ACTIVE' else 'crimson'
    ax.text(0.01,y,name,fontsize=10)
    ax.text(0.30,y,motif,fontsize=11,family='monospace')
    ax.text(0.55,y,spacing,fontsize=9)
    ax.text(0.86,y,verd,fontsize=10,weight='bold',color=col)
    y-=0.22
ax.text(0.01,0.02,"Canonical PTP P-loop = C-X5-R (invariant Arg 5 residues after catalytic Cys, with mid-loop Gly).\n"
        "Both auxilins have C-X3-R: the transition-state-stabilizing Arg is displaced -> no phosphatase activity.",
        fontsize=8.5,color='dimgray')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig('ploop_comparison.png',dpi=130,bbox_inches='tight')
print("saved")
